h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Oh, nearly get it: `14` is the parser's stop sign, not automatically the broken bit.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to struggle with wanting to fix the line where the parser stopped.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner only plans and declares their intention to use the new schema, but does not execute the actual classification task within the transcript.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
“Stop sign” is the right working label.

Use this gate before naming a suspect:

```text
Can an exact visible character or unfinished structure be pointed to in 12-14?

A: yes -> name it: bracket / quote / block / operator
B: no -> write: not enough context
```
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a structured decision gate to resolve the learner's confusion about when to declare 'not enough context'.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner only plans and declares their intention to use the new schema, but does not execute the actual classification task within the transcript.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: The narrow wording helps because it stops me from naming a person the line does not prove.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to ask for clarification on specific edge cases for labeling the margin under a tight deadline.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner only asks questions and seeks confirmation about rules without performing any new analysis or task on actual text.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor maintains the same scaffolding approach throughout the dialogue without any major strategy reversal or new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to work through the classification of headers and values under the tutor's guidance.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: I can fill the first two blanks: `city = Paris`, `rainfall_mm = 12`.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the task of filling in the blanks based on the header/value distinction.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not change their pedagogical strategy as there is no breakdown or pressure from the learner.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation occurs.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to place the force cards step-by-step as instructed by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Table on cart, rubbing. If the motion strip points right, this arrow goes left. Is left the side for this card?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner applies the physical rule that rubbing force opposes the direction of motion to determine the arrow's direction.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The learner proceeds smoothly without any breakdown or resistance, so no tutor reversal is triggered.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so recognition-contingent tutor uptake is not applicable.

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, the middle test only counts after the five cards are in numerical order.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to follow the tutor's instructions to find the median.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [writes the capped-marker number on the median line]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully performs the final step of writing the correct median number on the report line.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep both marks visible. The paperclip shows why the typed row looked usable; the capped marker shows the median rule. Before it goes into the summary, do the bench check: name the marked number, then count the two below and two above it on the sorted strip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor adapts to the learner's confusion by keeping both marks visible to contrast them and introducing a new bench-check verification step.
